title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 — Practice Test 2
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 0.5 + 0.25 + 0.125?
How many small squares make up the shaded shape shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 160" font-family="sans-serif"><g fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="40" y="40" width="30" height="30"/><rect x="70" y="40" width="30" height="30"/><rect x="100" y="40" width="30" height="30"/><rect x="40" y="70" width="30" height="30"/><rect x="100" y="70" width="30" height="30"/><rect x="40" y="100" width="30" height="30"/><rect x="70" y="100" width="30" height="30"/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A cross-like shape made of unit squares arranged in a grid.
questions.solution: Counting the shaded unit squares in the figure gives 7 squares.

questions.question_text: Five friends line up by height. Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Carl. Dan is taller than Anna. Eve is the tallest. Who is in the middle?
questions.solution: Order from tallest: Eve, Dan, Anna, Ben, Carl. Anna is third, the middle.

questions.question_text: What is 1/4 of 80 plus 1/5 of 50?
A baker sells muffins in boxes of 6. If she bakes 50 muffins, how many full boxes can she sell?
questions.solution: 50 divided by 6 is 8 remainder 2. She can fill 8 full boxes.

questions.question_text: What is the perimeter of the rectangle shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 240 160" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="40" y="50" width="160" height="60" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="115" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" fill="currentColor">8 cm</text><text x="210" y="85" font-size="14" fill="currentColor">3 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with length 8 cm and width 3 cm.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2(8+3) = 22 cm.

questions.question_text: Which number is divisible by both 3 and 5?
questions.solution: 45 = 3 x 15 = 5 x 9, so divisible by both.

questions.question_text: In a box there are 3 red balls and 4 blue balls as shown. You draw one ball without looking. Which color are you more likely to draw?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 240 120" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="20" y="40" width="200" height="60" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="50" cy="70" r="10" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="75" cy="70" r="10" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="100" cy="70" r="10" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="130" cy="70" r="10" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="155" cy="70" r="10" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="180" cy="70" r="10" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="205" cy="70" r="10" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/></svg>
questions.image_description: A box containing 3 red balls and 4 blue balls.
questions.solution: 4 blue > 3 red, so blue is more likely.

questions.question_text: What is 7 x 8 - 6 x 5?
How many triangles are in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 220 180" font-family="sans-serif"><polygon points="110,30 40,150 180,150" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="110" y1="30" x2="110" y2="150" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle with a line from the apex to the midpoint of the base.
questions.solution: The big triangle plus two smaller triangles gives 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: A number is multiplied by 3, then 5 is added, giving 26. What is the original number?
Three children say: Ava says 'I am not the youngest.' Bea says 'I am the oldest.' Cara says 'Ava is older than Bea.' Only one is telling the truth. Who is the oldest?
questions.solution: Test each case. If only Cara is truthful, Ava > Bea and Ava is youngest (so Bea not oldest). Then Cara must be oldest. Consistent.

questions.question_text: The big square has side 12. Four identical small squares are cut from the corners, each with side 3. What is the area of the remaining shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 200" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="30" y="30" width="140" height="140" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="35" height="35" fill="white" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="135" y="30" width="35" height="35" fill="white" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="30" y="135" width="35" height="35" fill="white" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="135" y="135" width="35" height="35" fill="white" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large square with four small squares removed from each corner.

questions.question_text: A train leaves at 9:45 as shown on the clock and travels for 2 hours 35 minutes. At what time does it arrive?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 160 160" font-family="sans-serif"><circle cx="80" cy="80" r="60" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="80" y1="80" x2="80" y2="35" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="80" y1="80" x2="50" y2="95" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="80" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="currentColor">12</text><text x="135" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="currentColor">3</text><text x="80" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="currentColor">6</text><text x="25" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="currentColor">9</text></svg>

questions.question_text: The ratio of cats to dogs in a shelter is 3:5. If there are 24 animals total, how many dogs are there?
questions.solution: Total parts = 8. Each part = 24/8 = 3. Dogs = 5 x 3 = 15.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest positive integer greater than 50 that has exactly three divisors?
questions.solution: Numbers with exactly 3 divisors are squares of primes. 49 = 7x7 is below 50. Next is 11x11 = 121.

questions.question_text: In how many ways can three different books be arranged on a shelf?
An equilateral triangle and a square have the same perimeter. The triangle's side is 12. What is the square's side?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 260 140" font-family="sans-serif"><polygon points="50,110 110,110 80,40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="160" y="50" width="60" height="60" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
questions.image_description: An equilateral triangle next to a square.
questions.solution: Triangle perimeter = 36. Square side = 36/4 = 9.

questions.question_text: Four cards show numbers 2, 4, 6, 8 as shown. Lucy picks two different cards. How many different sums are possible?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 260 100" font-family="sans-serif"><g fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="20" y="20" width="50" height="60" rx="4"/><rect x="80" y="20" width="50" height="60" rx="4"/><rect x="140" y="20" width="50" height="60" rx="4"/><rect x="200" y="20" width="50" height="60" rx="4"/></g><text x="45" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" fill="currentColor">2</text><text x="105" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" fill="currentColor">4</text><text x="165" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" fill="currentColor">6</text><text x="225" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" fill="currentColor">8</text></svg>

questions.question_text: What is 2/3 of 3/4 of 60?
Five friends each made one true statement. A: 'B lies.' B: 'C lies.' C: 'D lies.' D: 'E lies.' E: 'A and B both lie.' How many actually told the truth?
questions.solution: Try A true: B lies, so C true, D lies, E true. E says A and B both lie; but A told truth, contradiction. Try A lies: B true, C lies, D true, E lies. Check E: claims A and B both lie, but B told truth, consistent that E lies. Truth tellers: B and D = 2.

questions.question_text: The average of five numbers is 20. If one number 30 is removed, what is the average of the remaining four?
questions.solution: Total = 100. Remove 30, sum = 70. Average = 70/4 = 17.5.

questions.question_text: Three identical rectangles are arranged to form the shape shown. Each rectangle is 8 by 3. What is the total perimeter of the outline?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 260 180" font-family="sans-serif"><g fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="40" y="30" width="80" height="30"/><rect x="40" y="60" width="80" height="30"/><rect x="40" y="90" width="80" height="30"/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three rectangles stacked vertically forming a larger rectangle.
questions.solution: Combined shape is 8 wide by 9 tall. Perimeter = 2(8+9) = 34.

questions.question_text: Max has twice as many stickers as Liam. After Max gives Liam 6 stickers, they have equal numbers. How many did Max originally have?
questions.solution: Let Liam = x, Max = 2x. After: Max = 2x-6, Liam = x+6. 2x-6 = x+6, so x = 12. Max had 24.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all two-digit numbers whose digits add up to 5?
questions.solution: Numbers with digits summing to 5: 14, 23, 32, 41, 50. Sum = 14+23+32+41+50 = 160.

questions.question_text: A password uses three different digits from {1,2,3,4,5} and the middle digit must be even. How many different passwords are possible?
questions.solution: Middle: 2 choices (2 or 4). First: 4 remaining. Last: 3 remaining. 2 x 4 x 3 = 24.

questions.question_text: If 0.2 of a number equals 0.15 of 80, what is the number?
A cube is painted on all faces, then cut into 27 small cubes. How many small cubes have exactly two painted faces?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 180" font-family="sans-serif"><g fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><polygon points="50,60 110,40 170,60 170,130 110,150 50,130"/><line x1="110" y1="40" x2="110" y2="150"/><line x1="50" y1="60" x2="170" y2="60"/></g></svg>
questions.solution: Edge cubes (not corners) have exactly 2 painted faces. A cube has 12 edges, each contributes 1 middle cube. Total = 12.

questions.question_text: Six teams play in a round-robin tournament where every team plays every other team exactly once, as shown by the diagram. How many games are played in total?
questions.image_description: Six teams arranged in a hexagon with all pairs connected.
questions.solution: 6 choose 2 = 15 games.

questions.question_text: A farmer has chickens and cows. Together they have 20 heads and 56 legs. How many chickens are there?
questions.solution: Let c = chickens, w = cows. c+w=20, 2c+4w=56. Subtract: 2w = 16, w=8, c=12.
